name: Heck v. Humphrey
summary: Held that a Section 1983 damages claim is not barred by habeas requirements as long as the claim does not necessarily imply the invalidity of the conviction or sentence.
significance: Justice Sotomayor invoked this line of cases to argue that Section 1983 and Section 2255 can coexist as long as the civil suit does not challenge the validity of the conviction, analogizing to the relationship between compassionate release and habeas.
